items.title: Associated Press starts offering buyouts to newspaper journalists amid wider AI transformation
items.summary: The News Media Guild, the union that represents AP journalists, said more than 120 staff members received buyout offers on Monday.

items.title: OpenAI asks California, Delaware to investigate Musk's 'anti-competitive behavior' ahead of April trial
items.summary: OpenAI sent a letter to the California and Delaware attorneys general, urging them to investigate "anti-competitive behavior" by Elon Musk and his associates.

items.title: FDA declines to issue specific guidance on health programs' foreign drug imports
items.summary: A CNBC investigation revealed the proliferation of AFPs, which import prescription drugs from overseas in what U.S. regulators say is a violation of import laws.

items.title: S&P 500 aims to extend win streak — plus, a CFO switch at one of our key names
items.summary: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

items.title: Insurers Get 2.5% Medicare Advantage Rate Increase Bigger Than Earlier Flat Proposal
items.summary: The Trump administration announced a 2.48% payment increase for Medicare Advantage plans for 2027, a much bigger boost than an earlier proposal that kept rates flat.

items.title: Sam Altman says AI superintelligence is so big that we need a ‘New Deal’—critics say OpenAI’s policy ideas are a cover for ‘regulatory nihilism’
items.summary: OpenAI’s sweeping vision for the AI economy spans everything from public wealth funds to shorter workweeks—but critics say it raises familiar ideas without offering a clear path to action.

items.title: AI may replace your financial advisor, MIT professor says — but there's one big hurdle
items.summary: Artificial intelligence has gotten sophisticated with respect to financial advice. But it doesn't have a legal obligation to put clients first.

items.title: Braves Chose Walt Weiss As Manager Citing Experience And Continuity
items.summary: Walt Weiss was one of nine managers hired by MLB clubs this season, but among only three that had previous experience managing in the Major Leagues.

items.title: Trump is seeking the biggest defense budget surge in 75 years as the Pentagon stays committed to ‘exquisite’ weapons
items.summary: “A global security environment that is less reliant on norms and more reliant on force continues to put upward pressure on defense spending.”

items.title: U.S. Denies Iran’s Claim It Struck USS Tripoli, With 3,500 On Board
items.summary: Rumors circulated online that the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps targeted the ship carrying about 3,500 U.S. troops.

items.title: Deepfakes Could Break Overworked, Underfunded Public Defenders
items.summary: In the deepfake era, proving digital evidence is real requires experts public defenders cannot afford. Their indigent clients are the ones who will pay.

items.title: 10 Years Later, How T-Mobile Arena Paved A Path For Las Vegas Sports
items.summary: As the NBA considers Las Vegas expansion, how the 10th anniversary of T-Mobile Arena's opening carved a way for professional sports in Las Vegas

items.title: AI angst mutates into ‘FOBO’ as Fear of Becoming Obsolete takes over American workforces
items.summary: EY’s Joe Depa says he sees a split in staff. There’s “high adoption, right out of the gate” for juniors, but senior workers “are somewhat resistant.”

items.title: Team USA Hockey Hero Jack Hughes Shows Off His New Smile
items.summary: Jack Hughes is finally flashing his new smile in public after getting his front teeth busted out during Team USA’s gold medal victory over Canada at the Milan Cortina Olympics in February.

items.title: ‘No one’s raising their hand’: Japan’s labor crisis is making the case for robots taking the jobs you don’t want
items.summary: Japan is looking to become the dominant source of robotics by 2040, expedited by an aging population and a growing need for labor.
